description_long: This is a straight, low brick wall constructed from reddish-brown bricks arranged in a uniform horizontal pattern. The bricks are of standard size and are laid in a running bond, with visible mortar joints between each row. The wall has a consistent height and thickness along its entire length, with no visible openings or decorative features. The surface of the bricks appears slightly rough and matte, with some natural color variation and texture typical of fired clay. The top edge of the wall is flat and even, suitable for supporting small objects. This type of wall is commonly found in urban environments as a boundary, divider, or edging for sidewalks, gardens, or public spaces.
description: A straight, reddish-brown brick wall with a flat top and uniform running bond pattern. The surface is slightly rough and matte, typical of urban boundary walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the brick wall with its running bond pattern and flat top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and consistent thickness of the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same brick pattern and coloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow edge and uniform thickness.
